checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=309,230,000 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=370,340,000 (FY2009) = 83.5% margin; GrossProfit=466,846,000 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=620,255,000 (FY2012) = 75.3% margin
CostOfGoodsSold=61,110,000 (FY2009) grew to CostOfGoodsSold=153,409,000 (FY2012), a 151% increase versus 67.5% revenue growth

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=61,932,000 (Q4 2010) followed by NetIncomeLoss=-67,552,000 (Q3 2011) and NetIncomeLoss=-10,036,000 (Q4 2011)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=4,163,000 (2009-12-31) representing 7.1% of AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=58,462,000 (2009-12-31); by 2012, AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2,780,000 (2012-12-31) representing only 3.1% of AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=88,958,000 (2012-12-31)
AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=25,989,000 (2010-12-31) declined to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=20,880,000 (2012-12-31) while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew from 478,237,000 (FY2010) to 620,255,000 (FY2012)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Multiple SEC UPLOAD/CORRESP filings in 2010 (2010-07-30, 2010-08-11, 2010-08-20, 2010-08-30, 2010-09-03, 2010-09-28, 2010-10-12, 2010-10-28) indicating an SEC comment-letter review cycle
A second round of SEC UPLOAD/CORRESP filings occurred in 2012 (2012-09-14, 2012-09-27, 2012-10-18)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data series span from FY2007/2008 (e.g., StockholdersEquity=196,578,000 (2007-12-31)) through Q1 2013 (e.g., AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=90,410,000 (2013-03-31)) with both annual and quarterly granularity

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential under-reserving: allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of gross receivables fell from ~7.1% (2009) to ~3.1% (2012) even as receivables grew, and accrued liabilities fell in absolute terms (from $25.99M in 2010 to $20.88M in 2012) while revenue grew ~30% over the same period. This pattern is consistent with reserve releases or under-accrual being used to support reported net income/receivables rather than reflecting improved collections or lower risk.

Large divergence between reported net income and operating cash flow (e.g., FY2011 net loss of -$69.85M vs positive operating cash flow of $62.97M; FY2012 net income of only $3.14M vs operating cash flow of $130.08M) suggests substantial non-cash charges (e.g., litigation accruals, contingent consideration remeasurement, impairment, or amortization related to acquisitions) were recorded through the income statement without corresponding cash outflows in the same period, shifting the timing of expense recognition relative to cash reality.

Unusually large one-time swings in quarterly net income (a $61.93M spike in Q4 2010 versus normal quarterly income in the low single-digit millions, followed by a -$67.55M loss in Q3 2011) not proportionate to revenue changes suggest large one-off gains/charges (potentially related to litigation settlement, acquisition purchase accounting, or asset remeasurement) that could mask underlying recurring operating performance; concurrently, goodwill nearly grew 55% in one year (2010 to 2011) and 'other noncurrent assets' more than tripled from 2009 to 2012, raising questions about the recoverability/valuation support for these soft assets.

top_signals: Persistent large divergence between net income and operating cash flow in FY2011 (net loss -$69.85M vs +$62.97M OCF) and FY2012 (net income $3.14M vs +$130.08M OCF)
Declining allowance-for-doubtful-accounts and accrued-liabilities ratios relative to growing receivables/revenue, suggesting possible reserve under-funding

Two distinct rounds of SEC comment-letter correspondence (UPLOAD/CORRESP filings in 2010 and again in 2012) plus a 10-K/A amendment shortly after the FY2009 10-K, indicating elevated regulatory scrutiny

Highly volatile quarterly net income with large one-off swings (e.g., +$61.9M in Q4 2010, -$67.6M in Q3 2011) not proportionate to revenue trends, alongside rapid non-revenue-proportional growth in goodwill and other noncurrent assets
